prompt: scaffold a new go binary file library package for the gzip format. call it gzip.

assertions.text: gzip/doc.go exists
gzip/types.go exists
gzip/types_test.go exists
gzip/decoder.go exists
gzip/decoder_test.go exists
gzip/encoder.go exists
gzip/encoder_test.go exists
gzip/CLAUDE.md exists
go build ./gzip/... exits cleanly
go test ./gzip/... exits cleanly with all tests passing
decoder.go defines `func Decode(r io.Reader) (*File, error)`
encoder.go defines `func Encode(w io.Writer, f *File) error`
decoder.go and encoder.go store byte order as `binary.ByteOrder` field
test files call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
test files import github.com/stretchr/testify/require
gzip/CLAUDE.md documents byte order, error-wrapping convention, and testing style
types.go defines `errUnimplemented` (or equivalent) so the chain has a leaf to assert against
types.go defines an OffsetError type with Offset int64 and Err error fields, implementing Unwrap()
types.go defines a FieldError type with Field string and Err error fields, implementing Unwrap()
decoder.go defines an unexported counting reader that tracks bytes-read offset
encoder.go defines an unexported counting writer that tracks bytes-written offset
decoder.go has a wrapErr helper or equivalent that produces FieldError → OffsetError → leaf chain
encoder.go has a wrapErr helper or equivalent that produces FieldError → OffsetError → leaf chain
test files use errors.Is/ErrorIs to assert errUnimplemented AND errors.As/ErrorAs to assert the FieldError field path
gzip/CLAUDE.md explains the FieldError → OffsetError → leaf error-chain convention
